revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Tokiwa Jinja
常磐神社 | image = Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | width = | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | type = | dedication = | founded = 1874 | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, [[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki]] | phone = | website = http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html }} {{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second [[daimyō]] of the [[Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[Kōdōkan]] [[han school]].{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} In 1882 the shrine joined the ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Modern shrine ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events - May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[Cultural Properties of Japan|Cultural Properties]] by the city.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} [[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|right|200px|[[Tōrō]] toppled by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]] ==See also== {{Commonscategory|Tokiwa Jinja}} * [[Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== * {{ja icon}} [http://komonsan.jp/ Tokiwa Jinja - homepage] * {{en icon}} [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja - English summary] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Japan-struct-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Mito Domain]] [[ja:常磐神社]]

Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second [[daimyō]] of the [[Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[Kōdōkan]] [[han school]].{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011}}{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} In 1882 the shrine joined the ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Modern shrine ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events - May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[Cultural Properties of Japan|Cultural Properties]] by the city.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}} [[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|right|200px|[[Tōrō]] toppled by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]] ==See also== {{Commonscategory|Tokiwa Jinja}} * [[Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== * {{ja icon}} [http://komonsan.jp/ Tokiwa Jinja - revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3530470]] Wikidata name: Tokiwa Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tokiwa Jinja | image = File:Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3746|N|140.456|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qid=Q1194944}}; {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|qid=Q1323770}} | founded_by = | established = 1874 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, Mito-shi, Ibaraki-ken | website = {{URL|http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokiwachō|qid=Q11481592}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3530470]] Wikidata name: Tokiwa Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tokiwa Jinja | image = File:Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3746|N|140.456|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|qid=Q1194944}}; {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|qid=Q1323770}} | founded_by = | established = 1874 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, Mito-shi, Ibaraki-ken | website = {{URL|http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokiwachō|qid=Q11481592}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]

{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category|Tokiwa Jinja}} * {{Official website|http://komonsan.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} * [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja – English summary] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |date=6 June 2014 }} {{in lang|en}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category|Tokiwa Jinja}} * {{Official website|http://komonsan.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} * [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja – English summary] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |date=6 June 2014 }} {{in lang|en}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}
\

}}

Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[co:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[co:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530470]] Wikidata name: Tokiwa Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tokiwa Jinja | image = File:Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3746|N|140.456|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944}}; {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|WD=Q1323770}} | founded_by = | established = 1874 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, Mito-shi, Ibaraki-ken | website = {{URL|http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokiwachō|WD=Q11481592}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category|Tokiwa Jinja}} * {{Official website|http://komonsan.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} * [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja – English summary] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |date=6 June 2014 }} {{in lang|en}}

Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[co:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[co:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:توكيوا جينجا]][[sn:commons:Category:Tokiwa Jinja]][[en:Tokiwa shrine]][[eu:Tokiwa santutegia]][[fa:معبد توکیوا]][[fr:Tokiwa-jinja]][[ja:常磐神社]][[sv:Tokiwa-jinja]][[zh:常磐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3530470]] Wikidata name: Tokiwa Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tokiwa Jinja | image = File:Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3746|N|140.456|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944}}; {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|WD=Q1323770}} | founded_by = | established = 1874 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, Mito-shi, Ibaraki-ken | website = {{URL|http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokiwachō|WD=Q11481592}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are [[:en:Tokugawa Mitsukuni|Tokugawa Mitsukuni]], second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] and compiler of [[:en:Dai Nihonshi|Dai Nihonshi]], and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby [[:en:Kōdōkan|Kōdōkan]] [[:en:Han school|Han school]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural Properties]] by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''[[:en:Stone lantern|Stone lantern]]'' toppled by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake]]]]
==See also== * [[:en:Mito Domain|Mito Domain]] * [[Tokugawa clan]] * [[:en:Kōdōkan (Mito)|Kōdōkan]] * [[:en:Tōshō-gū|Tōshō-gū]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category|Tokiwa Jinja}} * {{Official website|http://komonsan.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} * [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja – English summary] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |date=6 June 2014 }} {{in lang|en}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

Domain|WD=Q1146904|lt=Mito Domain}} and compiler of {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihonshi}}, and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=Cultural Properties}} by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>

{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tokiwa Jinja | image = File:Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3746|N|140.456|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944}}; {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|WD=Q1323770}} | founded_by = | established = 1874 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, Mito-shi, Ibaraki-ken | website = {{URL|http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokiwachō|WD=Q11481592}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}, second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the {{ill|Mito Domain|en|Mito Domain|WD=Q1146904|lt=Mito Domain}} and compiler of {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihonshi}}, and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby {{ill|Kōdōkan|en|Kōdōkan|WD=Q1797576|lt=Kōdōkan}} {{ill|Han school|en|Han school|WD=Q3578015|lt=Han school}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=Cultural Properties}} by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''{{ill|Stone lantern|en|Stone lantern|WD=Q12012294|lt=Stone lantern}}'' toppled by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake}}]]
==See also== * {{ill|Mito Domain|en|Mito Domain|WD=Q1146904|lt=Mito Domain}} * [[Tokugawa clan]] * {{ill|Kōdōkan (Mito)|en|Kōdōkan (Mito)|WD=Q3081773|lt=Kōdōkan}} * {{ill|Tōshō-gū|en|Tōshō-gū|WD=Q1190456|lt=Tōshō-gū}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category|Tokiwa Jinja}} * {{Official website|http://komonsan.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} * [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja – English summary] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |date=6 June 2014 }} {{in lang|en}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=Cultural Properties|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''{{ill|Stone lantern|en|Stone lantern|WD=Q12012294|lt=Stone lantern|1=Stone lantern}}'' toppled by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}]]
==See also== * {{ill|Mito Domain|en|Mito Domain|WD=Q1146904|lt=Mito Domain|1=Mito Domain}} * [[Tokugawa clan]] * {{ill|Kōdōkan (Mito)|en|Kōdōkan (Mito)|WD=Q3081773|lt=Kōdōkan|1=Kōdōkan (Mito)}} * {{ill|Tōshō-gū|en|Tōshō-gū|WD=Q1190456|lt=Tōshō-gū|1=Tōshō-gū}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]

{{Use dmy dates|date=August 2022}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Tokiwa Jinja | image = File:Tokiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | caption = [[Haiden (Shinto)|Haiden]] | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.3746|N|140.456|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}; {{ill|Tokugawa Nariaki|WD=Q1323770|1=Tokugawa Nariaki}} | founded_by = | established = 1874 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3-1 Tokiwa-cho, Mito-shi, Ibaraki-ken | website = {{URL|http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Tokiwachō|WD=Q11481592|1=Tokiwachō}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Tokiwa Jinja'''|常磐神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} adjacent to the [[:en:Japanese gardens|gardens]] of [[:en:Kairakuen|Kairakuen]] in [[Mito, Ibaraki]], Japan. Founded in 1874, enshrined are {{ill|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|en|Tokugawa Mitsukuni|WD=Q1194944|lt=Tokugawa Mitsukuni|1=Tokugawa Mitsukuni}}, second ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of the {{ill|Mito Domain|en|Mito Domain|WD=Q1146904|lt=Mito Domain|1=Mito Domain}} and compiler of {{ill|Dai Nihonshi|en|Dai Nihonshi|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihonshi|1=Dai Nihonshi}}, and [[Tokugawa Nariaki]], ninth lord and founder of the nearby {{ill|Kōdōkan|en|Kōdōkan|WD=Q1797576|lt=Kōdōkan|1=Kōdōkan}} {{ill|Han school|en|Han school|WD=Q3578015|lt=Han school|1=Han school}}.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |title=Tokiwa Jinja Shrine |publisher=Tokiwa Jinja |accessdate=19 May 2011 |archive-date=6 June 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/html/english/spots/tokiwa.htm |title=Tokiwa Shrine |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> In 1882 the shrine joined the Ranks of the {{nihongo|''bekkaku kanpeisha''|別格官幣社}} or [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Imperial Shrines]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=1092 |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto – Modern shrine Ranking system |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/images/uploads/EOS070712Eb.pdf |title=List of kanpeisha |publisher=[[Kokugakuin University]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> The Tokiwa Jinja Reisai or [[Japanese festivals|annual festival]] is held on 12 May.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.tobunken.go.jp/english/events/5-e.html |title=List of Annual Events – May |publisher=[[National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Tokyo]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref> A cannon and a drum have been designated as {{ill|Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q858308|lt=Cultural Properties|1=Cultural Property (Japan)}} by the city.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/view.rbz?nd=1379&of=1&ik=1&pnp=1373&pnp=1374&pnp=1379&cd=1479 |title=Mito bunkazai |publisher=[[Mito, Ibaraki|Mito City]] |accessdate=19 May 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Monuments fell down by 2011 Tōhoku Earthquake in Tokiwa-shrine.JPG|thumb|''{{ill|Stone lantern|en|Stone lantern|WD=Q12012294|lt=Stone lantern|1=Stone lantern}}'' toppled by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=March 2011 Tōhoku earthquake|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}]]
==See also== * {{ill|Mito Domain|en|Mito Domain|WD=Q1146904|lt=Mito Domain|1=Mito Domain}} * [[Tokugawa clan]] * {{ill|Kōdōkan (Mito)|en|Kōdōkan (Mito)|WD=Q3081773|lt=Kōdōkan|1=Kōdōkan (Mito)}} * {{ill|Tōshō-gū|en|Tōshō-gū|WD=Q1190456|lt=Tōshō-gū|1=Tōshō-gū}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category|Tokiwa Jinja}} * {{Official website|http://komonsan.jp}} {{in lang|ja}} * [http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html Tokiwa Jinja – English summary] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606182014/http://komonsan.jp/for_english/tokiwa_jinja_shrine.html |date=6 June 2014 }} {{in lang|en}}
